objectives: Develop comprehensive language skills (grammar, spelling, writing, pronunciation, and vocabulary).
Expand general knowledge in sports, history, civilization, and culture.
Promote quick thinking and smart decision-making.
Encourage healthy competition and challenge-based learning.
Help players discover their mistakes and improve their true language level.
Support inclusion and integrate visually impaired students into the learning experience.
Combine education with fun and entertainment.
Integrate technology into learning through educational resources via QR codes.
Promote sustainable learning beyond the game experience.

behind.title: Behind the Scenes
body: We are students of the specialized translation program in the Spanish language, Department of Hispanic Languages and Literatures, Cairo University, and we graduate in 2026.
2S (Si Sabes) is our graduation project — a game that challenges the illusion of perfection in language learning.

The name carries a creative idea: when you repeat '2S' several times, it sounds like the number twelve. The number twelve symbolizes perfection, and we often believe our level in a language is already complete and solid.

That's where 2S comes in. Our game invites you to test yourself in Spanish, English, and Arabic in a fun and interactive way. It doesn't just measure what you know — it reveals your true level while helping you grow.
Our goal is to teach new linguistic ideas, cultural aspects, and important language concepts in a simple, engaging, and easy-to-remember way.
We believe learning should not be boring. It should challenge you, surprise you, and stay in your mind.

2S is designed for everyone, and we are proud to support inclusion by integrating accessibility tools for people with visual impairments, while promoting language, culture, and the exchange of knowledge.
Because knowing… is only the beginning.

howToPlay.title: How to Play
howToPlay.p1: Draw a card from the question deck (1–50). You will find two questions in the available languages on the card, and you must answer only one question on your turn.
howToPlay.p2: Choose the question whose answer you know or the one that seems easier to you, and answer it to earn its points.
howToPlay.p3: If your answer is correct → draw a card from the Eye of Horus deck (cards 51–75).
howToPlay.p4: If your answer is incorrect → draw a card from the Curse deck (cards 76–100).

supportTools.title: Support Tools
supportTools.p1: At the bottom of each card there is a QR code that you can scan to listen to the question in audio, which makes the game easier for visually impaired players.
supportTools.p2: There is also another QR code next to each question that directs you to a website with general information to help you learn more about the topic of the question, but it does not necessarily contain the direct answer to the question you chose.

winner.title: Winner
winner.p1: You are the winner if you collect the highest number of points when the question cards run out.
